description: Segel — confidential sealed-bid OTC desk on Stellar. Two BN254 Groth16 ZK circuits (bidValidity, auctionResult) verified on-chain by Soroban; an otc desk contract that escrows and settles in Circle's real testnet USDC (SAC), with Vickrey settlement, per-RFQ nullifiers, a live Reflector price oracle, and two-asset delivery-vs-payment (the maker escrows a sell-side lot delivered to the winner against payment).

token.note: Circle's CANONICAL testnet USDC (issuer GBBD47IF…, ~38k holders) via its SAC. The desk escrows and settles in real Circle USDC: each bidder's good-faith escrow (= band_max) is custodied here, winner pays clearing to maker, losers refunded. Migrated from the earlier project-issued mock (GC7SWGHR…) via upgrade() + set_token (see tokenMigration).

bidValidity.proves: commit binding + in-band range + proof-of-funds + ASP allow-list membership + fresh nullifier

auctionResult.proves: all commitments bind + winner = argmax + clearingPrice = second-highest (Vickrey), all in-circuit; losing bids never output
auctionResult.tamperedResult: rejected on-chain when the clearing-price public input is flipped — reproducible live in one command (npm run test:tamper-onchain / scripts/tamper-onchain.mjs): a real proof verifies on the DEPLOYED verifier (true), then the SAME proof with a tampered clearing fails the on-chain Groth16 pairing check ('pairing product did not equal identity'). Soundness checked live, not asserted

directedRfqUpgrade.note: Direct OTC can now target ONE counterparty: post_rfq_dvp takes an optional `taker` (Some(address) = only that address may bid, enforced in commit_bid with Error #15 NotInvitedTaker; None = open to anyone). New `taker(rfq_id)` view. To stay within Soroban's 10-argument-per-function cap, the DvP lot's 3 params were bundled into one `BaseSpec {token, amount, symbol}` struct (an earlier 11-arg version made the contract spec unparseable by the JS SDK — caught + fixed). Backward-compatible (additive Taker key; existing RFQs read taker()=None). Verified live: directed RFQ #22, taker() round-trips, a non-invited commit_bid rejected #15.

dvpHardeningUpgrade.note: Post-DvP-audit hardening + oracle-as-sanity-circuit-breaker, applied live via upgrade() (SAME id), then re-audited and made fail-safe. (M1) post_rfq_dvp rejects base_token == quote token, so the sell-side lot can't commingle with bidders' escrow. (M3) the winner's base delivery is now NON-reverting (try_transfer): a winner who can't receive the lot is credited a base-claimable and pulls it via claim_base() — settle never bricks. (Oracle) set_price_guard(bps) arms a Reflector circuit-breaker: settle reads the live mark and rejects a clearing total outside ±bps of the lot's market value (mark_price(base) * base_amount). HONEST SCOPE (corrected after a second audit found the original 'load-bearing security' framing overclaimed): it's a fat-finger / honest-misconfig net, not anti-manipulation — the maker supplies the band + symbol, so the auctionResult proof remains the real binding on clearing. A follow-up upgrade made the guard FAIL-OPEN (skip, never panic/brick) on no feed / zero-or-negative price / overflowing product. post_rfq_dvp gains base_symbol. Backward-compatible (additive storage; pre-upgrade RFQs have no base symbol and skip the guard).

dvpUpgrade.note: Two-asset delivery-vs-payment (DvP) added live via upgrade() — SAME contract id, fully backward-compatible (existing quote-only RFQs untouched). post_rfq_dvp lets a maker escrow a sell-side lot (a second SAC, e.g. native XLM) up front; settle delivers that lot to the winner atomically against the winner's USDC payment, and cancel_expired returns it to the maker. The leg key is cleared on delivery/refund, so base_leg() reads null afterward. The desk is now a real swap, not a one-sided payment.

hardeningUpgrade.note: Post-audit security hardening applied live via upgrade() — SAME contract id, no redeploy. Fixes: reserve=band_min (a lone bidder can no longer clear at 0); un-griefable refunds (settle/cancel use a non-reverting transfer and credit a claimable balance on failure, so a bidder who drops their USDC trustline can't lock escrow) + a claim() pull entrypoint; checks-effects-interactions (status written before any transfer).

tokenMigration.note: Migrated the desk to Circle's real testnet USDC WITHOUT a redeploy — proves the new upgrade() path. Same contract id, live site unaffected.
tokenMigration.to: Circle canonical testnet USDC SAC CBIELTK6…

oracleIntegration.usage: Consumed in the settle money-path (not merely advisory): mark_price(symbol) does a real cross-contract lastprice() read, and when the admin arms set_price_guard(bps), settle REJECTS a clearing total outside ±bps of the DvP lot's market value (mark_price(base) * base_amount). HONEST SCOPE: a fat-finger / honest-misconfiguration circuit-breaker, NOT an anti-manipulation control — the maker supplies the band + base symbol, so an adversarial maker can widen/skip it; the auctionResult proof is the real binding on clearing. Fails OPEN (never bricks settle) on no feed / zero price / overflow, and cancel_expired bypasses it, so it can never lock funds. Also surfaced read-only in the UI Audit tab + as an MCP tool.
oracleIntegration.priceGuard: armed at 5000 bps (±50%) on the live contract; verified live: a 4.20 USDC clearing against a ~3.45 USDC mark (XLM 0.1724 * 20) settled within band
oracleIntegration.priceGuardProofOnChain: simulated live against RFQ #12 (post-upgrade DvP, 1 bid) with the guard tightened to 500 bps: settle at 5.00 USDC (off-market) -> rejected Error(Contract,#14) OutOfOracleBand BEFORE proof verification; settle at 3.40 USDC (near-market) -> passes the guard and only fails proof (#0) on the dummy proof used. Demonstrates the oracle read genuinely gates the clearing in the money path (not dead code). Guard restored to 5000 bps after.

security.binding: the contract builds every verifier public-input vector itself from typed values it controls (authenticated bidder, RFQ band, recorded commitments) — a proof cannot be reused with different inputs
security.proofOfFunds: commit_bid pins availBal = band_max (the escrow) and then transfers that escrow from the bidder; the transfer must succeed, so the bidder provably holds >= band_max >= bid (the hidden bid is bound to escrowed funds)
security.vickreyBinding: settle builds the auction public inputs from the RECORDED commitments (padded to N=8 with Poseidon(0,0,0)), so the proof is over exactly the on-chain sealed set
security.nullifier: one bid per identity per RFQ (Sybil resistance), identity hidden
security.reserve: settle requires clearing >= band_min, so a single-bidder auction (Vickrey runner-up = padded 0) can no longer clear at 0
security.refundSafety: settle/cancel_expired refund via a NON-reverting transfer; a failed refund (e.g. a bidder dropped their USDC trustline) is credited as a claimable balance instead of bricking the whole batch — pulled later via claim(). One bidder cannot DoS settlement or the cancel cleanup
security.adminControls: admin-gated set_verifiers (rotate a buggy verifier — previously immutable), set_admin (hand over the role), set_oracle (swap the price feed), set_token (migrate the escrow asset), and upgrade (in-place WASM upgrade, keeps the contract id); instance-storage TTL is extended on every state change so config cannot expire
security.dvp: post_rfq_dvp escrows the maker's sell-side lot at post (must be a DIFFERENT asset than the quote token, enforced — else lot and escrow would commingle); settle delivers it to the winner atomically against the USDC payment; cancel_expired returns it to the maker. Delivery is NON-reverting: a winner who can't receive is credited a base-claimable (claim_base()) so settle never bricks. clearing is the TOTAL quote for the whole lot (per-lot pricing, not per-unit). Delivery/refund clears the leg key (no double-delivery; base_leg() reads null after)
security.oracleGuard: when armed (set_price_guard bps>0), settle additionally requires the clearing total to sit within ±bps of mark_price(base)*base_amount — the Reflector oracle is consumed in the money path, not merely advisory. Fail-OPEN (skips, never bricks) when off / no base symbol / no feed / non-positive price / overflowing product; cancel_expired bypasses it. Honest scope: a fat-finger net, NOT anti-manipulation (the maker controls the band + symbol); the auctionResult proof is the real clearing binding
security.directedOtc: post_rfq_dvp's optional `taker` invites one counterparty; commit_bid rejects anyone else (#15 NotInvitedTaker). `taker(rfq_id)` view; absent = open to anyone. Lets Direct OTC be a real bilateral trade, not just a label
security.unitTests: 29/29 passing (contracts/otc/src/test.rs); DvP + base≠quote guard + base-claimable fallback + oracle price-guard (allow + reject) + directed-OTC (invited bids / outsider rejected / open accepts anyone) all covered

e2eLive.description: Full sealed-bid OTC flow verified live on testnet in REAL Circle USDC (scripts/e2e-testnet.mjs): post RFQ -> 3 sealed bids with real bidValidity proofs -> Vickrey settle. Winning bid 4.90 USDC, clearing price 4.20 USDC (second-highest of 4.90/4.20/3.80); losing bids never revealed on-chain. Escrow + payouts are Circle's canonical testnet USDC. (Superseded by e2eLiveDvp below, which adds the two-asset delivery leg + oracle guard.)

e2eLiveDvp.description: Full TWO-ASSET DvP flow verified live on testnet WITH the Reflector oracle price-guard armed (±50%) (scripts/e2e-testnet.mjs): maker escrows a 20 XLM sell-side lot at post_rfq_dvp -> 3 sealed USDC bids with real bidValidity proofs -> Vickrey settle delivers the 20 XLM to the winner against their USDC payment, after settle reads the live Reflector mark and confirms the 4.20 USDC clearing is within band of the ~3.45 USDC lot value. Verified by reading the desk's XLM SAC balance: it rises by 20 at post and falls by 20 at settle (delivery proven on-chain), and the script throws if the lot is not delivered.

circuits.trustedSetup: Powers-of-Tau 2^14 (phase-1 generated locally for the hackathon build; production wants the Hermez perpetual ceremony + multi-party phase-2)
